answer.text: The change in option premium for a one-unit change in the price of the underlying asset
The rate at which the option premium decays over time
The sensitivity of the option premium to changes in volatility
The change in option premium due to changes in interest rates

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Delta (Δ) = Change in Option Price / Change in Underlying Price
Delta tells you how much the option premium will change when the underlying moves by Re. 1.
**The Trap:** Theta = time decay. Vega = volatility sensitivity. Rho = interest rate sensitivity. Don't confuse the Greeks.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Delta is the most important and most frequently used Greek for hedging.
**Key Takeaway:** Delta = sensitivity of option price to underlying price change. Most important Greek.
